note:
作为Facebook Connect活动的一部分，今天发布的Move健身跟踪器概述了该公司未来的增强现实和虚拟现实计划，这是Facebook试图加入健身和健康跟踪热潮的尝试，这股热潮为佩洛顿和Zwift等公司带来了数亿美元和数十亿美元的估值。
在此之前，苹果公司昨天宣布了一项名为Fitness+的健身锻炼服务。
这是一种让Oculus用户检查他们在Beats Saber或专用健身应用程序中燃烧了多少卡路里的方式。Oculus Move仪表盘将允许Quest和Quest 2的所有者通过他们的VR应用程序跟踪他们的健身目标。使用移动功能，Quest佩戴者可以设置日常健身目标，检查他们燃烧了多少卡路里，以及他们在佩戴耳机时活动了多长时间。
这一新功能将使Facebook的Quest耳机更多地与Tonal、F45、Strava、Zwift和peloton等健身硬件和软件公司处于同一领域。
这一功能将很快推出，并将在Quest 2中提供，目前正在接受预订。随着该公司的新挑战功能也将于今天推出，Facebook无疑是在寻求将健身硬件和软件初创公司放在其Quest设备的视线内。
note_en:
Announced today as part of the Facebook Connect event outlining the company’s augmented reality and virtual reality plans for the future, the Move fitness tracker is an attempt by Facebook to get in on the fitness and health tracking craze that’s yielded hundreds of millions of dollars and billions in valuation to companies like Peloton  and Zwift.
 The announcement follows  Apple’s own announcement yesterday of a fitness workout service called Fitness+.
   It’s a way for  Oculus   users to check on how many calories they’re burning in  Beat Saber or in dedicated fitness apps. The Oculus Move dashboard will allow Quest and Quest 2 owners to keep track of their fitness goals across their VR apps. Using the Move feature, Quest wearers can set daily fitness goals, check how many calories have been burned and how long they’ve been active while wearing the headset.
 The new feature will put Facebook’s Quest headsets more in the same realm as fitness hardware and software companies like Tonal, F45, Strava, Zwift and Peloton.
 The feature will be rolled out soon and will be available in the Quest 2, which is taking pre-orders now. With the company’s new challenges feature that’s also rolling out today, Facebook is definitely looking to put the fitness hardware and software startups in the sights of its Quest devices.
title: Facebook发布了Oculus Move，这是一项健身跟踪功能，将于今年晚些时候推出Quest，它可以帮助跟踪任何应用程序消耗的卡路里，并带有游戏内的覆盖功能
title_en: Facebook unveils Oculus Move, a fitness tracking feature coming to Quest later this year that helps track calories burned across any app, with in-game overlays

note:
跳到主要内容作为一个写虚拟现实的人，在2020年大流行期间和许多人一样被困在家里，我被一次又一次地问到一个问题：如果虚拟现实如此伟大，而现实现实如此危险，为什么不每个人都转向VR呢？这并不是一个完全公平的框架，因为虚拟现实的使用在某些方面已经激增。但它确实暴露了一个简单的问题：我真的不能推荐一款对大多数人来说足够友好的耳机。
不过，在过去的几周里，我一直在使用Oculus的新Quest 2耳机，这一点几乎要改变了。Quest 2是一款独立的耳机，将于10月13日发货，它是Oculus 2019年Quest的更新。Oculus保留了独立的Quest设计，具有相同的功能集，同时改进了屏幕，减轻了重量，并-值得一提的是-让它变得更舒适。它还将起价从399美元降至299美元，使Quest 2成为市场上价格最低的耳机之一。
任务2是我在最初的任务发布时所喜欢的一切，但得益于过去一年发展起来的更强大的生态系统。尽管当前一代的虚拟现实存在固有的笨拙，但它感觉像是最终产品，而不是早期采用者的实验。Oculus是社交巨头Facebook旗下的一家公司，到目前为止已经做了一些最好的工作。这也引发了一些关于虚拟现实未来的最大问题。
乍一看，Quest 2看起来像是一款戏剧性的新耳机。Facebook旗下的Oculus以其全黑色旗舰设备而闻名，但Quest 2拥有纯白的机身和黑色的泡沫面膜，使其外观具有双色调。新的配色方案不一定是一种改进，但它是有意义的：Facebook已经将其VR产品线整合到一款设备中，并使该设备在视觉上与众不同。
任务2在其他方面看起来与任务非常相似。这款耳机比上一款略小，但两款耳机都有相同的圆形塑料正面，每个角落都镶嵌着一个外向式跟踪摄像头。Quest有纹理面料的侧面，而全塑料的Quest 2机身感觉更无菌，尽管这被取代Quest硬质橡胶头带的柔软面料头带所抵消。Quest 2的控制器是白色的，但在其他方面与前几代Oculus Touch设备几乎相同。
你不会从Quest2中获得很大的新功能，但它包含了一些重要的迭代升级。这款耳机的内存为6 GB，而不是4 GB。它的基本型号有64 GB的存储空间，就像Quest一样；花399美元，你可以得到一个大大扩展的256 GB的存储空间。Quest 2使用高通的骁龙XR2芯片组，而不是骁龙835，假设让它运行更好看的游戏(尽管Quest 2的当前库针对原始耳机进行了大量优化，两款设备都没有达到PC的能力)，同时利用了针对跟踪摄像头和VR屏幕分辨率等XR2的特定优化。这款耳机仍然很笨重，但与Quest的571相比，它只有503克，只比相对轻的原始Oculus Rift略重一点。电池续航时间与Quest相似，游戏时间在2至2.5小时之间。
Quest 2的屏幕分辨率已经超越了大多数其他VR头戴式耳机，每只眼睛提供1832x1920像素，而最初的是1440x1600；Oculus还承诺在发布后将次优的72 Hz刷新率升级到90 Hz。分辨率并不是唯一重要的因素-Valve Index拥有旧Rift的分辨率，但其更大的视场和更高的刷新率使屏幕变得宽广和清晰。即使有了更新，Quest 2的屏幕与Quest的屏幕也没有明显的不同，但文字明显没有那么粗糙，反过来，整个体验感觉更精致。
最初的Oculus Quest前部重得可怜，即使是更苗条的Quest 2也会让你的额头沉重。它柔软的布带几乎感觉像是往下走了一步，支撑较少，收紧机构也略显笨拙。不过，Oculus还提供了另一种表带选择：一个带衬垫的塑料环，它更容易套在你的头上，并在后面用方便的轮子拧紧。
这并不像像PlayStation VR这样的固定耳机那样万无一失。我最终会有一个完全可以接受的长达几个小时的疗程，然后再进行另一次，我不断地摆弄角度，让自己感到舒服。但这确实是一个进步--我的丈夫在“任务2”中只忍受了几分钟，就通过了“节拍军刀”的竞选模式。这也比试图用第三方配件修改“任务”要简单，因为你只需在两边各打开一个塑料插座来交换表带即可。
可悲的是，Oculus并没有改变我最不喜欢的Quest功能：它使用的是小型定向扬声器，而不是耳机或耳塞。问题不在于音质；Quest2的音质还算不错，虽然我觉得它有点薄。那就是在同一个房间里的任何人都能听到声音，而且我的耳机和耳机都不能很好地配合使用。有线耳机需要将长线系在带子后面，而Oculus显然不打算在短期内支持无线耳机-Quest 2团队的负责人Prabhu Parthasarathy表示，延迟是一个太大的问题。相反，罗技正在销售定制长度的有线耳机和耳麦，价格分别为50美元和100美元。
以VR耳机的标准来看，Quest 2便宜得离谱，即使它进行了几次升级。但令人有点沮丧的是，默认情况下，Oculus没有包括改进后的表带和耳塞，因为新的耳机用户不一定会意识到他们的体验会有多好。与智能手机附带的充电器和廉价耳机不同，人们肯定不会在家里到处漂浮这些配件。
早期的Quest 2泄密事件引发了人们的担忧，即Oculus可能会放弃焦距调整-移动耳机的镜头以匹配不同的瞳孔间距离的选项，从而改善更多用户的体验。Oculus没有做到这一点，但它让调整过程变得更加烦人。你不必移动耳机底面的平滑滑块，只需取下滑块，将镜头按到三个距离设置中的一个，然后再重新戴上，就能看到改进情况。Oculus表示，这一选择将帮助新来者了解调整的作用，但好处尚不清楚。虽然单个用户可能会设置此选项并将其忘记，但如果您要在家庭成员之间或跨组织交换耳机，这将是一个更大的问题。
另一方面，控制器感觉一如既往地好。Oculus没有改变其第一代Touch建立的界面：你仍然会得到一对塑料遥控器，有一个握柄按钮和一个触发器，两个面部按钮，每个按钮上都有一个模拟杆。然而，Quest 2的设计者重新引入了第二代Touch中去掉的拇指靠背，解决了我对外部设计的唯一不满。
在内部，每个控制器仍然使用一个AA电池，而不是一些VR控制器中的内置充电器。Parthasarathy说，这是因为没有办法不尴尬地向他们收费，尽管Oculus可能会在某个时候过渡。Quest可以在坚固的使用一到两周内消耗掉电池，但Oculus承诺Quest 2控制器已经过优化，续航时间是原来的四倍。虽然我使用的时间不够长来证实这一点，但我的显示，在大量使用一周后，它们的电量达到了90%。
Oculus在最初的Quest发布后引入了手部跟踪功能，Quest 2在发布时就可以使用这一功能。这是主屏幕的一个很好的额外选项，让你无需操控控制器就可以调整设置或安装游戏。它还在慢慢推广到第三方应用程序，包括工作场所社交工具Spatial-这是一个很好的低压测试用例，你不需要完全可靠的快速抽搐动作。
和Quest一样，Quest 2的手部识别也很准确，而且Oculus信誓旦旦地承诺，随着时间的推移，它会不断改进。但是，像捏手掌或转动手掌这样的手势可能会被意外触发，也可能无法注册，而且当你用手移动光标时，它并不总是指向你预期的位置。因此，就目前而言，它更多的是一种额外的好处，而不是一个完整的附加界面。
任务2的硬件，至少在备用表带上，几乎在各个方面都改进了任务。它的生态系统已经走了很长的路。独立的Quest目录一开始有大约50款游戏，Oculus承诺在Quest 2发布前后超过200款。
杰出的游戏BEAT Saber正在不断扩大新歌的下载量，最近一次是从Linkin Park下载，尽管与摇滚乐队等非VR节奏游戏相比，它的目录仍然很小。它还将在这个假期获得多人模式和BTS歌曲套装。即将推出的游戏包括：由卢卡斯电影特效工作室ILMxLAB创作的“星球大战：来自银河边缘的故事”；“狙击手精英”的VR部分；一款名为“侏罗纪世界余波”的侏罗纪公园益智游戏；一款以战锤4万宇宙为背景的射击游戏；以及一款由VR改编的“神秘”。
这些游戏不提供审查，但现有的Quest目录已经包括了一些相对较新的宝石。维达不朽三部曲将一个令人愉快的故事与令人满意的光剑训练模式配对。“房间VR：黑暗物质”为“房间”系列中引人注目的谜题带来了更加亲身实践的体验。隐形游戏“魅影：秘密行动”以一个巨大、黑暗、低对比度的世界推动了任务的权力和屏幕的极限，但它聪明的自负--你正在潜入秘密基地，从皮划艇上刺杀敌人--很容易弥补这一点。
在这些当代游戏中，只有幻影是Oculus耳机独有的。Facebook在发布时看起来有相当大的独家阵容，但它也在宣传Quest 2的跨平台潜力。这包括即将推出的战斗皇室游戏Population：One，将于2020年秋季发布，以跨HTC Vive、Windows混合现实和Oculus耳机的交叉玩为特色。Quest 2的一些展示应用程序甚至不是只支持VR的Spatial，它也支持智能手机和桌面Web应用程序。
去年，随着Link的推出，Quest得到了巨大的推动。Link是一项功能，可以让它通过USB-C连接玩PC VR游戏。Link消除了Quest的主要牺牲：无法访问特别图形密集型的Oculus游戏、SteamVR等第三方店面，以及没有进入Quest高度精心策划的平台的体验。更具体地说：它让任务运行了迄今为止最好的虚拟现实游戏之一“半条命：阿利克斯”(Half-Life：Alyx)。
LINK自去年以来一直处于测试阶段，但在Quest 2发布前后，它正在成为官方功能。虽然今年春天我表达了对该系统的一些失望，但它在Quest 2上运行得很好，部分原因是官方的Link电缆比Anker Powerline等便宜得多的USB-C选项有了改进。(不幸的是，它在Quest 2的成本上又增加了79美元。)。Link电缆长达5米，明显长于平均长度，其插头成角度整齐地贴在耳机上。一根无角度的Anker电缆很容易在最初的Quest上松动，尽管Quest 2还将其USB-C端口移到耳机上更靠后的位置，这可能会改善两款耳机的整体体验。再加上任务2改进后的屏幕，我并没有发现自己错过了Oculus Rift。我最大的障碍就是再次习惯系绳耳机。
Oculus Quest 2保留了当前一代VR的基线缺陷：与现代手机或笔记本电脑相比，它的颗粒状、笨重和社交笨拙。目前，VR在某些特定的流派中也是最好的。你可以找到各种各样的节奏游戏，射击游戏，虚拟拳击等模拟运动，以及社交的虚拟世界。相比之下，360度视频是一个人烟稀少的领域，尽管像Felix&Amp；Paul这样的制片厂都在做这方面的工作。
但是，如果你对当前一代的VR游戏感兴趣，Quest 2提供了硬件、功能和价格的最佳整体平衡。(即使有升级的表带、Link电缆和耳塞，这款独立产品的价格也低于许多PC系留耳机。)。它最直接的竞争对手是HTC Vive Focus Plus和Pico Neo 2等以商务为重点的耳机，除非索尼或苹果等公司宣布推出独立的耳机。
VR开发商已经制作了一些可靠的主播游戏，包括Beat-Saber，Half-Life：Alyx，The Room VR，僵尸射手亚利桑那州阳光，以及BigScreen VR等社交空间。晕车仍然是VR中的一个问题，但随着开发人员学到了更好的设计技巧，耳机跟踪技术也得到了改进，找到不会引发晕车的体验变得更容易了。
如果你已经有了Quest，Quest 2不是一个必须升级的时刻--目前宣布的所有Quest 2游戏都可以在Quest上运行，Facebook目前不允许开发者发布Quest 2的独家内容。但硬件有明显的好处。如果你有一个老式的PC耳机，比如原来的Rift或Vive，Quest就是一个很大的进步。
Quest是第一款需要Facebook账户的Oculus耳机，而不仅仅是一个独立的Oculus账户。如果你是这个平台的新手，你可以注册Facebook。如果您拥有Oculus帐户，系统将提示您合并帐户。
这不是听起来像是翻天覆地的变化。Facebook已经拥有它通过Oculus收集的所有信息，包括一些可以预测的数据(你的应用程序使用历史)和一些不太明显的数据(你如何在你的游戏空间上划定界限)。版主可以交叉参考姓名和IP地址，以找到给定Facebook用户的Oculus账户。你可以在这两个平台上保留一个单独的朋友列表，而且你的个人资料仍然会显示你创建的VR头像。虽然公司可以根据你玩的游戏定向投放Facebook新闻馈送广告，但没有耳机内广告或虚拟现实新闻馈送(VR News Feed)。
我合并了我现有的Facebook和Oculus账户来审查这款耳机，我的Oculus体验完全相同。我不经常查看Facebook，但根据最近浏览的News Feed，它目前正试图向我推销远程教育和Nextdoor--而不是最新的VR射击游戏。
即便如此，加强Oculus和Facebook之间的正式联系也引发了新的担忧。很多人意外地从Facebook被停职，如果发生这种情况，还不清楚它会对Oculus设备产生什么影响。该公司的新使用条款表明，如果你做一些事情，比如用假名创建Facebook账户，你可能会失去访问内容的权限，但Oculus表示，细节仍在制定中。Parthasarathy说，Facebook正试图在适度的情况下取得平衡，所以短暂的暂停不会破坏你的Oculus图书馆。但Facebook的节制规模如此之大，以至于个人用户可能会被困在系统中，失去对您购买的游戏和硬件的访问权限的前景是令人恐惧的。
而Facebook拥有某些东西的技术能力，与默认情况下这样做是不同的。版主已经可以因为严重的Facebook违规行为而禁止Oculus账户，但这需要额外的步骤。从理论上讲，Facebook以前可以将VR活动与社交媒体账户联系起来，但展望未来，它会自动将一组全新的数据点添加到已经非常庞大的行为目录中。
虚拟现实是一种令人难以置信的亲密和潜在的侵入性媒介。例如，正如“虚拟现实之路”(Road To VR)上个月概述的那样，Facebook仅限邀请的Horizon社交空间包括一个选项，可以让主持人在看不见的情况下监视你与另一个人的对话，看是否有潜在的违规行为。这是标准游戏审核做法的延伸-例如，索尼和微软可以让你举报辱骂的私人信息。但Facebook的运营规模要大得多，对分析你的行为并将其货币化有更明显的兴趣。
此外，许多人可能不会费心使用Link电缆或游戏机。这给Oculus批准的游戏子集带来了很大的优势，这些游戏的选择不仅是因为质量，而且是因为人们认为商业上的可行性。Facebook已经讨论过为更广泛的应用程序开设一个限制较少的商店，但目前拒绝提供更多细节，建议开发人员如果想要试验，就为PC开发。这人为地限制了许多游戏的观众，这些游戏在移动耳机上运行得很好。一些社交VR开发商已经在抱怨Facebook压制竞争，而Quest 2只会增加它设定合约条款的权力-可能还会增加耳机用户看到的游戏种类。
与任务2的具体好处相比，这些可能看起来像是抽象的问题。但Quest2是我可以合理地向广大观众推荐的第一款耳机。Facebook的虚拟现实领先地位正在增长，来年可能会设定业界对隐私、开发者自主权和基本的消费者友好性的期望。这可能不是虚拟现实的大众市场时刻，但现在是开始认真思考这个世界可能如何运作的时候了。

note_en:
Skip to main content                                                                                                                As someone who writes about virtual reality and has been stuck at home just like many people during the 2020 pandemic, I’ve gotten asked one question over and over:  if virtual reality is so great and actual reality is so dangerous, why isn’t everybody turning to VR? This isn’t a totally fair framing, since VR usage  has spiked by  some  measures. But it did expose a simple problem: I really couldn’t recommend a headset that was friendly enough for most people to buy.
 I’ve spent the last couple of weeks with Oculus’ new Quest 2 headset, though, and that’s very close to changing. The Quest 2 is a self-contained headset that’s shipping on October 13th, and it’s an update to  Oculus’ 2019 Quest. Oculus has kept that standalone Quest design with the same feature set, while improving its screen, reducing its weight, and — with one noteworthy caveat — making it more comfortable. It’s also dropped the starting price from $399 to $299, making the Quest 2 one of the lowest-priced headsets on the market.
 The Quest 2 is everything I liked about the original Quest at launch but with the benefit of a stronger ecosystem that’s  developed over the past year. Even with current-generation VR’s inherent awkwardness, it feels like a final product rather than an early-adopter experiment. Oculus — a company owned by social giant Facebook — has done some of its best work so far. It’s also provoked some of the biggest questions yet about VR’s future.
  At a passing glance, the Quest 2 looks like a dramatically new headset. Facebook-owned Oculus has become known for its all-black flagship devices, but the Quest 2 has a pure white body and a black foam face mask, giving it a two-toned appearance. The new color scheme isn’t necessarily an improvement, but it makes sense: Facebook has consolidated its VR product line into a single device, and it’s making that device visually distinct.
 The Quest 2 looks otherwise very similar to the Quest. It’s a little smaller than its predecessor, but both headsets have the same rounded plastic front, each corner studded with an outward-facing tracking camera. The Quest had textured fabric sides, while the all-plastic Quest 2 body feels a little more sterile, although that’s offset by soft fabric head straps that replace the Quest’s stiff rubber ones. The Quest 2’s controllers are white, but they’re otherwise nearly identical to previous generations of Oculus Touch devices.
  You won’t get big new features from the Quest 2, but it includes some significant iterative upgrades. The headset features 6GB of memory instead of 4GB. Its base model has 64GB of storage, just like the Quest; for $399, you can get a greatly expanded 256GB. The Quest 2 uses Qualcomm’s Snapdragon XR2 chipset instead of a Snapdragon 835, hypothetically letting it run better-looking games (although the Quest 2’s current library is heavily optimized for the original headset, and neither device approaches a PC’s power) while taking advantage of  XR2-specific optimization for things like tracking cameras and VR screen resolution. The headset remains chunky, but at 503 grams compared to the Quest’s 571, it’s only a little heavier than the relatively lightweight original Oculus Rift. Battery life is similar to the Quest, clocking between two and 2.5 hours for a gaming session.
 The Quest 2’s screen resolution has leapfrogged most other VR headsets, offering 1832 x 1920 pixels per eye compared to the original’s 1440 x 1600; Oculus also promises to upgrade the suboptimal 72Hz refresh rate to 90Hz after launch. Resolution isn’t the only factor that matters — the Valve Index has the old Rift’s resolution, but its larger field of view and higher refresh rate make the screen expansive and crisp. Even with the update, the Quest 2 screen isn’t transformationally different from the Quest’s. But text is notably less grainy, and in turn, the whole experience feels more polished.
  The original Oculus Quest was miserably front-heavy, and even the slimmer Quest 2 can weigh on your forehead. Its soft cloth strap almost feels like a step down, with less support and a slightly clumsier tightening mechanism. Oculus is also offering an alternate strap option, though: a padded plastic ring that rests more easily around your head and tightens with a convenient wheel at the back.
 This isn’t as foolproof as some tethered headsets like the PlayStation VR. I’ll end up having one perfectly acceptable hours-long session and then another where I fidget constantly with the angle to make myself comfortable. But it’s a definite step up — my husband, who could only stand the Quest for a few minutes, got through a solid chunk of  Beat Saber’s campaign mode in the Quest 2. It’s also simpler than trying to  mod your Quest with a third-party accessory, since you just pop a plastic socket at each side to switch straps.
  Oculus sadly hasn’t changed my least favorite Quest feature: its use of small, directed speakers instead of headphones or earbuds. The problem isn’t the sound quality; the Quest 2 has reasonable audio, although I find it a little thin. It’s that the sound is audible to anybody in the same room, and none of my headphones work well with the headset. Wired ones need their long cords pinned behind the straps, and Oculus apparently isn’t planning wireless earbud support anytime soon — Quest 2 team lead Prabhu Parthasarathy says latency is too big an issue. Instead, Logitech is selling custom-length wired earbuds and over-ear headphones for $50 and $100, respectively.
 The Quest 2 is ridiculously cheap by VR headset standards, even with its handful of upgrades. But it’s a little frustrating that Oculus isn’t including the improved strap and earbuds by default, since new headset owners won’t necessarily realize how much better their experience could be. And unlike the charger and cheap headphones that ship with smartphones, people definitely won’t have these accessories floating around their home already.
 Early Quest 2 leaks prompted fears that Oculus might be ditching focus adjustment — the option to move a headset’s lenses to match different interpupillary distances, improving the experience for a wider range of users. Oculus hasn’t done this, but it’s made the adjustment process more annoying. Instead of moving a smooth slider on the headset’s underside, you have to remove it and snap the lenses to one of three distance settings, then put it back on to see the improvement. Oculus says this option will help newcomers understand what the adjustments do, but the benefit isn’t clear. And while a single user will probably just set this option and forget it, it’s a bigger issue if you’re swapping the headset between family members or across an organization.
  The controllers, on the other hand, feel as good as ever. Oculus hasn’t changed the interface it established with its first-generation Touch: you’ll still get a pair of plastic remotes with a grip button and a trigger, two face buttons, and an analog stick on each one. The Quest 2’s designers reintroduced a thumb rest that was removed from the second-generation Touch, though, fixing my only complaint with the external design.
 Internally, each controller still uses one AA battery, not the built-in rechargeables you’ll find in some VR controllers. Parthasarathy says this is because there’s no way of charging them that’s not awkward, although Oculus will probably transition at some point. The Quest could chew through batteries with a week or two of solid use, but Oculus promises the Quest 2 controllers have been optimized to last four times as long. While I haven’t been using it long enough to confirm that, mine indicated that they were at 90 percent power after a week of heavy use.
 Oculus introduced hand tracking after the original Quest’s release, and the feature is available at launch on the Quest 2. It’s a great extra option for the home screen, letting you tweak settings or install games without bothering with the controllers. It’s also slowly being rolled out to third-party apps, including the workplace social tool Spatial — a good, low-pressure test case where you don’t need perfectly reliable fast-twitch motion.
 Like the Quest’s, the Quest 2’s hand recognition is accurate, and Oculus credibly promises that it will improve over time. But gestures like pinching or turning your palm can be either accidentally triggered or fail to register, and when you’re using your hand to move a cursor, it doesn’t always point where you’d expect. So for now, it’s more of a perk than a full additional interface.
  The Quest 2’s hardware, at least with the alternate strap, improves the Quest in almost every way. And its ecosystem has come a long way. The standalone Quest catalog started with around 50 titles, and Oculus promises over 200 around the Quest 2’s launch.
 Standout game  Beat Saber is being continuously expanded with new song downloads, most recently  from Linkin Park, although its catalog remains small compared to non-VR rhythm games like  Rock Band. It’s also getting a multiplayer mode and a BTS song pack this holiday. Upcoming games include  Star Wars: Tales from the Galaxy’s Edge, created by Lucasfilm effects studio ILMxLAB; a VR installment of  Sniper Elite; a  Jurassic Park puzzle game called  Jurassic World Aftermath;  a shooter set in the  Warhammer 40,000 universe; and a VR adaptation of  Myst.
 These games weren’t available for review, but the existing Quest catalog already includes some relatively recent gems. The  Vader Immortal trilogy pairs an enjoyable story with a satisfying lightsaber training mode.  The Room VR: A Dark Matter brings a more hands-on approach to the  Room series’ remarkably compelling puzzles. Stealth game  Phantom: Covert Ops pushes the limits of the Quest’s power and screen with a large, dark, and low-contrast world, but its clever conceit — you’re infiltrating secret bases and assassinating enemies  from a kayak — easily makes up for it.
  Of those current-generation titles, only  Phantom is exclusive to Oculus headsets. Facebook looks to have a sizable exclusive lineup at launch, but it’s also promoting the Quest 2’s cross-platform potential. That includes upcoming battle royale game  Population: One, which will be released in the fall of 2020 and features cross-play across HTC Vive, Windows Mixed Reality, and Oculus headsets. Some of the Quest 2’s showcase apps aren’t even VR-only — like Spatial, which also supports smartphones and a desktop web app.
 The Quest got a huge boost last year with Link, a feature that lets it play PC VR games with a USB-C connection. Link removed the Quest’s central sacrifice: the loss of access to particularly graphics-intensive Oculus games, third-party storefronts like SteamVR, and experiences that didn’t make it onto the Quest’s highly curated platform. In more concrete terms: it lets the Quest run  Half-Life: Alyx, one of the best VR games to date.
 Link has been in beta since last year, but it’s becoming an official feature around the Quest 2’s launch. While I  expressed some frustration with the system this spring, it worked great with the Quest 2, in part because the official Link cable is an improvement over a much cheaper USB-C option like the Anker Powerline. (It unfortunately adds another $79 to the cost of a Quest 2.) The Link cable is significantly longer than average at five meters, and its plug is angled to sit neatly against the headset. A non-angled Anker cable would easily jiggle loose on the original Quest, although the Quest 2 also moves its USB-C port farther back on the headset, which could improve the overall experience for both. Add in the Quest 2’s improved screen, and I didn’t find myself missing the Oculus Rift. My biggest hurdle was simply getting used to a tethered headset again.
  The Oculus Quest 2 retains current-generation VR’s baseline flaws: it’s grainy, bulky, and socially maladroit compared to a modern phone or laptop. VR is also best in some specific genres right now. You can find a variety of rhythm games, shooters, simulated sports like virtual boxing, and virtual worlds in which to socialize. By contrast, 360-degree video is a more sparsely populated field, despite the work of studios like Felix & Paul.
 But if you’re interested in the current generation of VR games, the Quest 2 offers the best overall balance of hardware, features, and price. (Even with the upgraded strap, Link cable, and earbuds, this standalone product costs less than many PC-tethered headsets.) Its closest direct competition are business-focused headsets like the HTC Vive Focus Plus and Pico Neo 2, unless a company like Sony or Apple announces a standalone headset.
 And VR developers have produced some solid anchor titles, including  Beat Saber,  Half-Life: Alyx,  The Room VR, zombie shooter  Arizona Sunshine, and social spaces like Bigscreen VR. Motion sickness can still be a problem in VR, but as developers have learned better design tricks and headset tracking has improved, it’s become easier to find experiences that don’t trigger it.
 If you’ve already got a Quest, the Quest 2 isn’t a must-upgrade moment — all currently announced Quest 2 games will work with the Quest, and Facebook isn’t letting developers ship Quest 2-exclusive content at this time. But the hardware has distinct benefits. And if you’ve got an older PC-tethered headset, like an original Rift or Vive, the Quest is a big step up.
   The Quest is the first Oculus headset to require a Facebook account, not just a standalone Oculus account. If you’re new to the platform, you’ll sign up with Facebook. If you’ve got an Oculus account, you’ll be prompted to merge your accounts.
 This isn’t the sea change it might sound like. Facebook already owned all the information it collected through Oculus, including some data that’s predictable (your app usage history) and some that’s less obvious (how you draw the boundaries on your play space.) Moderators could cross-reference names and IP addresses to find a given Facebook user’s Oculus account. You can keep a separate friends list on the two platforms, and your profile still shows a VR avatar that you create. There’s no in-headset advertising or VR News Feed, although companies can target Facebook News Feed ads based on the games you play.
 I merged my existing Facebook and Oculus accounts to review this headset, and my Oculus experience was exactly the same. I don’t regularly check Facebook, but based on a recent scroll through the News Feed, it’s currently trying to sell me on distance learning and Nextdoor — not the latest VR shooter.
  Even so, tightening the formal links between Oculus and Facebook raises new concerns. Lots of people get accidentally suspended from Facebook, and if that happens, it’s not clear how it affects Oculus devices. The company’s  new terms of use suggest you can lose access to content if you do something like make a Facebook account with a fake name, but Oculus says the details are still being worked out. Parthasarathy says Facebook is trying to strike a balance in moderation, so a brief suspension won’t nuke your Oculus library. But Facebook moderation happens on such a huge scale that individual users can get stuck in the system, and the prospect of losing access to your purchased games and hardware is a scary one.
 And Facebook having the technical capacity for something is different from doing it by default. Moderators could already ban Oculus accounts for egregious Facebook violations, but that required an extra step. Facebook could theoretically link VR activity to social media accounts before, but going forward, it’s automatically adding a whole new set of data points to an already vast catalog of your behavior.
 VR is an incredibly intimate and potentially invasive medium.  As  Road to VR outlined last month, for instance, Facebook’s invite-only  Horizon social space includes the option to have a moderator invisibly surveil your conversations with another person for potential rule-breaking. This is an extension of standard gaming moderation practices — Sony and Microsoft, for instance, let you report abusive private messages. But Facebook operates at a much greater scale and has a clearer interest in analyzing and monetizing your behavior.
 Also, many people may not bother with a Link cable or a gaming PC. That gives a big advantage to a subset of Oculus-approved games, which are selected not just for quality, but  for perceived commercial viability. Facebook has discussed opening a less restrictive store for a wider range of apps, but it declined to offer more detail at this time, suggesting that developers build for PC if they want to experiment. That artificially limits the audience for many games that could run just fine on a mobile headset. Some social VR developers are already complaining about  Facebook suppressing competition, and the Quest 2 only increases its power to set the terms of engagement — and potentially the kinds of games that headset users see.
 These might seem like abstract concerns compared to the Quest 2’s concrete benefits. But the Quest 2 is the first headset I can reasonably recommend for a wide audience. Facebook’s VR head start is growing, and the coming year could set industry expectations for privacy, developer autonomy, and basic consumer-friendliness. This may not be VR’s mass-market moment, but it’s the moment to start seriously thinking about how that world might work.

note: Oculus Quest 2在这里，听起来在任何方面都像是对原版的巨大升级。今天在Facebook Connect大会上宣布，Quest 2将具有比以前更清晰的显示屏，分辨率比以前高50%，支持平滑的90 Hz刷新率，入门价低得令人惊讶，只有299美元。基本上，Facebook似乎正在将Quest定位为终极VR头盔：一款可以无线将你推入虚拟现实世界的头盔，而不需要任何额外的硬件。对于那些有幸拥有一台游戏PC的人来说，这是一款功能强大的有线耳机。
note_en: The Oculus Quest 2 is here, and it sounds like a tremendous upgrade  over the original in every way. Announced today during the Facebook Connect conference, Quest 2 will feature sharper displays with a 50 percent higher resolution than before, support for smooth 90Hz refresh rates and a surprisingly low entry price of $299. Basically, it seems like Facebook is positioning the Quest to be the ultimate VR headset: One that can thrust you into VR worlds wirelessly, without the need for any extra hardware. And for those lucky enough to have a gaming PC, it’s a more than capable wired headset.

note_en:
This week American Airlines banned a passenger  within hours of a mask incident, something that  usually requires multiple violations at the carrier. In this case the boarding door had already closed and the jet bridge had been pulled back, when the customer who refused to wear a mask needed to be removed from the aircraft. They weren’t just violating mask rules,  they were compromising  D0!
 However it’s not just passengers refusing to wear personal protection equipment that’s a problem at the airline. It seems another problem is passengers  who wear too much. So last week American Airlines sent a memo to its customer care agents on “Prohibited Personal Protection Equipment (PPE).”
 There are several items that the airline does not allow, and deems ‘recreational’:
  Our customers can be creative when it comes to face coverings. Thanks to your photos and descriptions of customers’ Personal Protection Equipment (PPE), we want to share the types of “recreational” items and PPE that are not allowed on our flights. Specifically:
 Here are some tips on what to do if you see a customer using any non-approved mask or face shield:
 Politely remind them of our policy  Advise them that these types of PPE are safety concerns, especially tents / pods which can slow access to critical safety procedures, including emergency evacuations and use of oxygen masks
 We understand our customers may be concerned about being in close contact with other customers. This is a perfect time to remind them of the steps we’ve taken to keep them safe:
 Our Clean Commitment was launched to keep every gate area, jetbridge and aircraft clean  We’ve begun using new breakthrough SurfaceWise2 spraying solution  We require everyone to wear an approved mask or face covering to reduce the risk of virus transmission  We also offer extra wipes (where available) for customers to clean the area around them

note:
(Nanowerk新闻)Insight-HXMT团队对正在吸积的X射线脉冲星Gro J1008-57进行了广泛观测，发现中子星表面的磁场约为10亿特斯拉。
发表在“天体物理杂志快报”(Insight-HXMT)上的这项工作主要由中国科学院高能物理研究所和德国茨宾根埃伯哈德·卡尔斯大学的科学家共同完成，其中包括“GRO J1008-57&34；光谱中最高能量基本回旋共振散射特征的探测”(Insight-HXMT Firm Detect of the High-Energy Basic Cyclotron Resonance Feature in the Spectrum of GRO J1008-57&34；)，该研究由中国科学院高能物理研究所(IHEP)和德国茨宾根Eberhard Karls大学的科学家共同完成。
科学家们研究了Insight-HXMT在2017年8月爆发期间探测到的X射线脉冲星Gro J1008-57。他们首次发现了90keV的回旋共振散射特性，显著性水平为&gt；20σ(请注意，当显著性水平大于5σ时，科学界确认了一项新的科学发现)。
根据理论计算，这个CRSF对应的磁场高达10亿特斯拉，比地球实验室能产生的磁场强几千万倍。
洞察力-HXMT是中国第一颗X射线天文卫星。它包括一个高能望远镜，一个中能望远镜，一个低能望远镜和一个空间环境监测仪等科学有效载荷。与其他X射线卫星相比，Insight-HXMT具有光谱覆盖范围宽(1-250keV)、高能有效面积大、时间分辨率高、死区时间短、亮源堆积效应可忽略不计等优点，在回旋线(特别是高能)探测中具有突出的优势。
中子星拥有宇宙中最强的磁场。中子星X射线双星是由一颗中子星和一颗正常恒星伴星组成的系统。中子星吸积物质，形成周围的吸积盘。如果磁场很强，吸积的物质会被磁力线引导到中子星的表面，从而产生X射线辐射。
因此，这些源也被称为脉冲星。先前的研究表明，有时可以在X射线脉冲星的光谱中发现一种特殊的吸收特征(称为回旋共振散射特征)。科学家们认为，这是由于垂直于磁场的电子运动的离散朗道能级之间的跃迁造成的。这样的散射特征可以直接探测到中子星表面附近的磁场。
Insight-HXMT由IHEP于1993年提出，并于2017年6月成功发射。IHEP负责这颗卫星的科学有效载荷、地面部分和科学研究。
note_en:
( Nanowerk News) The Insight-HXMT team has performed extensive observations of the accreting X-ray pulsar GRO J1008-57 and has discovered a magnetic field of ~1 billion Tesla on the surface of the neutron star.
  This work, published in  The Astrophysical Journal Letters ( "Insight-HXMT Firm Detection of the Highest-energy Fundamental Cyclotron Resonance Scattering Feature in the Spectrum of GRO J1008-57"), was primarily conducted by scientists from the Institute of High Energy Physics (IHEP) of the Chinese Academy of Sciences and Eberhard Karls University of Tbingen, Germany.
 Scientists studied the X-ray pulsar GRO J1008-57 detected by Insight-HXMT during its outburst in August 2017. They discovered for the first time a cyclotron resonant scattering feature (CRSF) at 90 keV at a significance level of > 20 σ (Note that the scientific community confirms a new scientific discovery when its significance level is larger than 5 σ).
 According to theoretical calculations, the magnetic field that corresponds to this CRSF is up to one billion Tesla, which is tens of millions of times stronger than what can be generated in Earth laboratories.
 Insight-HXMT is the first Chinese X-ray astronomical satellite. It comprises scientific payloads including a high-energy telescope, a medium-energy telescope, a low-energy telescope, and a space environment monitor. Compared with other X-ray satellites, Insight-HXMT has outstanding advantages in the detection of cyclotron lines (especially at high energies) due to its broadband (1-250keV) spectral coverage, large effective area at high energies, high time resolution, low dead time and negligible pile-up effects for bright sources.
 Neutron stars have the strongest magnetic fields in the universe. Neutron star X-ray binaries are systems consisting of a neutron star and a normal stellar companion. The neutron star accretes matter and forms a surrounding accretion disk. If the magnetic field is strong, the accreted matter is channeled by magnetic lines onto the surface of the neutron star, resulting in X-ray radiations.
 As a result, these sources are also called "pulsars." Previous studies have shown that a peculiar absorption feature (known as a "cyclotron resonant scattering feature") can sometimes be found in the spectrum of X-ray pulsars. Scientists believe this is caused by transitions between the discrete Landau levels of electronic motion perpendicular to the magnetic field. Such a scattering feature acts as a direct probe to the magnetic field near the neutron star's surface.
 Insight-HXMT was proposed by IHEP in 1993 and was successfully launched in June 2017. IHEP is responsible for scientific payloads, ground segments and scientific research involving this satellite.
title: 宇宙中的最强磁场
title_en: Strongest Magnetic Field in the Universe

«回到博客主页自从Fusion 360进入市场的那一天起，我们就开始坚定地基于这样的理念来打造一款产品，即设计工具应该对每个人都是可用的，始终是最新的，并且易于使用。您和您的团队不应该受到您所在位置的限制，也不应该受到您运行的操作系统平台的限制。我们生活在未来，我们希望Fusion 360能带你走得更远。
今天，Fusion360涵盖了这一理念的方方面面，从头开始构建产品的最好的事情之一就是看到我们的社区在过去7年里难以置信的增长。一开始，和许多初创公司一样，我们的用户屈指可数。快进到现在，每个月都有645,000名用户在积极使用Fusion 360。你们向我们展示了它已经成为你们生活的一部分，为此，我们永远心存感激。
我们认识到，个人使用的Fusion 360使你们中的许多人能够将一个充满激情的项目转化为一个可行的收入来源，我们绝对希望继续支持你们做自己喜欢的事情。我们听到了关于这项服务将如何消失，或将不再免费的讨论，我们想借此机会说，我们对您和社区的承诺没有改变。个人使用服务对于那些只做基于家庭的、非商业性的设计、制造和制造项目的人仍然是免费的。
Fusion 360正在迅速成熟，它已经赢得了最实惠的端到端产品开发工具的声誉-我们希望保持这种状态。随着我们继续针对路线图更新的三大重点领域提供更多功能，我们还需要明确区分我们的个人使用产品和商业产品。在我们的社区论坛和社交媒体渠道上，我们看到产品之间的区别、免费产品和非免费产品之间的区别，以及有意/无意将个人产品误用于商业目的的困惑情绪不断重现。在我们的社区论坛和社交媒体渠道上，我们再次看到产品之间的区别，什么是免费的，什么不是，以及故意/无意地将个人产品用于商业目的的误用。
那我们就开始谈正事吧。我们的快速增长给我们带来了一系列需要集体克服的新挑战。“为了让我们作为专业产品开发和制造的首选统一工具引领市场，我们需要消除我们产品中存在的困惑和误用。”我们需要确保我们能够以可持续的方式进行扩展，并支持我们的专业订户的高级功能，同时忠于我们为每个人设计民主化的指导原则。考虑到这一点，我们将在Fusion 360可供个人使用的产品与其他产品之间定义更清晰的界限。
10个激活文档：今天，默认情况下，您的所有设计和文档都处于激活状态。您可以打开任何设计，无论是旧的还是新的，它们都会打开。您可以拥有尽可能多的文档选项卡。10个活动文档意味着您有10个位置可供任何文档在任何给定时间打开和编辑。您的所有其他文档将以新的存档状态存储在您的项目中。它们将保留在您的项目中，保存在您的项目中。您的帐户中可以有无限数量的存档文档(设计)和项目(数据面板中的顶级文件夹)。如果您使用了10个文档的容量，并且在任何时候想要重新访问较旧的设计并将其打开，则可以通过存档现有的活动文档并激活存档的文档来交换位置来实现此目的。
DXF：您仍可以在浏览器中的草图上单击鼠标右键，然后选择“另存为DXF”。
制造：2轴、2.5轴和3轴铣削仍将在新的个人使用产品中提供。
我们花了相当多的时间研究使用模式，并采访了各种各样的业余爱好者，最终目标是尽可能减少对绝大多数业余爱好者的干扰。有了这些改变，你仍然可以使用Fusion 360作为端到端的设计解决方案，从探索设计概念到3D打印和加法/减法制造。我们还将继续支持Fusion 360供个人使用，并定期进行产品更新，就像我们今天支持它的方式一样。
如果您目前正在使用Fusion 360进行个人使用，并且需要将您的设计导出为上述任何一种格式，您可以在2020年10月1日之前完成此操作。10份有效单据津贴将于2021年1月19日生效。在此日期之后，您可以选择要保持活动状态的10个文档，其余文档将以存档状态存储在您的帐户中。您可以随时将存档文档交换为活动文档。您选择保留的存档设计数量没有限制。
教育和创业产品保持原样，并可通过申请和验证流程获得。学生和教育工作者仍然可以免费获得一年的Autodesk产品和服务的教育访问权限，只要您仍然符合条件，这些产品和服务就可以续订。如果您已经在为Fusion 360付费，那么对您来说什么都不会改变。
如果您需要的某些特性或功能不再包含在Fusion 360中供个人使用，我们希望您有机会以折扣入门价获得完整版，无论是1年期订阅还是3年期订阅。
变化可能会让人不舒服，但如果没有变化，我们就不能作为一家企业成长，同时仍能为您和我们的社区提供行业领先的价值。如果我们不能成长，我们就会变得陈旧，如果我们变得陈腐，我们就不会再把Fusion 360做得最好。从第一天开始，我们的反馈就一直对Fusion 3360的整体产品战略和路线图方向起着举足轻重的作用。我们将尽我们所能继续支持您的旅程，我们衷心感谢您对这一变化的理解。
我们以为您有问题要问我们，所以我们整理了一份综合的常见问题清单。您可以通过下面的链接访问它。
您当地的经销商也可以帮助您回答您可能有的任何问题，所以也可以随时联系他们。
订阅以获取产品更新和增强功能、有用的Fusion 360提示和教程、路线图更新和社区故事。
note_en:
« Back to Blog home page          Since the day Fusion 360 entered the market, we set out to build a product firmly based on the philosophy that design tools should be available to everyone, always up-to-date, and easy to use. You and your teams should not be limited by where you’re located, nor by the OS platform you run on. We’re living in the future, and we want Fusion 360 to take you even further.
  Today,  Fusion 360 encompasses all aspects of this philosophy, and one of the best things about building a product from scratch is seeing the incredible growth of our community over the past 7 years. In the beginning, like  many startups, we only had a handful of users.  Fast forward to now,  and there are  645,000  of you actively using  Fusion 360   every month . Y ou’ve shown us how much  it  has become a part of your lives, and for that, we are eternally grateful.
    We recognize that Fusion 360 for personal use has enabled so many of you to turn a passion project into a viable source of income, and we absolutely want to continue to support you doing what you love. We hear discussions of how this offering is going away, or will no longer be free, and we want to take this opportunity to say that our commitment to you and the community has not changed. The personal use offering is still free for those of you solely doing home-based, non-commercial design, manufacturing, and fabrication projects.
  Fusion 360 is maturing at a rapid pace, and it has earned its reputation as the most affordable end-to-end product development tool out there—we want to keep it that way. As we continue to deliver more capabilities against the   three major areas of focus  in our roadmap update, we also need to have a clear distinction between our offerings for personal use and commercial use.  On our community forums and social media channels, we see a reoccurring sense of confusion of what is the difference between the offerings, what is free, what is not, and intentional/unintentional misuse of the personal offering for commercial purposes.
    So let’s get down to business. Our rapid growth presents a new set of challenges for us to collectively overcome.  In order for us to lead the market as the #1 unified tool-of-choice in professional product development and manufacturing, we need to eliminate both the confusion and the misuse that exists within our offerings.  We need to make sure that we can scale in a sustainable way and support advanced capabilities for our professional subscribers, while, at the same time, staying true to our guiding principles of democratizing design for everyone. With this in mind, we will be defining clearer lines between what is available for Fusion 360 for personal use versus the other offerings.
      10 active documents: Today all your designs and documents are active by default. You can open any design no matter how old or new and they will open. You can have as many document tabs as possible. 10 active documents means that you have 10 spots for any document to open and be editable at any give time. All your other documents will be stored in your projects in a new archived state. They will remain yours, saved in your projects. You can have unlimited number of archived documents (designs) as well as projects (top level folders in your data panel) in your account. If you are at your 10 document capacity, and at any point you want to revisit an older design and open it up, you can do so by archiving an existing active document and activating the archived one to swap places.
  DXF: You will still be able to right click on a sketch in the browser and select Save As DXF.
  Manufacture: 2, 2.5 and 3 axis milling will still be available in the new Personal Use offering.
  We spent considerable time studying usage patterns and doing interviews with a wide variety of hobbyists, with an end goal to have as little disruption as possible to the vast majority of the hobbyist community. With these changes, you  ’ll   still be able to use Fusion 360 as an end-to-end design solution, from   exploring design   concept  s   to 3  D   printing and additive/subtractive manufacturing. We’ll also continue to support Fusion 360 for personal use with regular product updates, just like how we are supporting it today.
    If you’re currently using Fusion 360 for personal use and need to export your designs out as any of the formats mentioned above, you have until October 1st, 2020 to do so. The 10 active document allowance will be in effect on January 19, 2021. After this date, you will have the option to select which 10 documents you would like to remain active, and the rest will be stored in your account in an archived state. You can swap an archived document for an active document at any time. There is no limit on how many archived designs you choose to keep.
  The Education and Startup offerings remain as-is and are available through an application and verification process. Students and educators can still get  free one-year educational access to Autodesk products and services, renewable as long as you remain eligible. If you’re paying for Fusion 360 already, nothing is changing for you.
  If there are specific features or functionality you need that are no longer included in Fusion 360 for personal use, we want to give you the opportunity to get the full version at a discounted entry price, either as a 1- or 3-year subscription.
      Change can be uncomfortable, but without change, we cannot grow as a business and still deliver industry-leading value to you and our community. If we can’t grow, we become stale, and if we become stale, we ’re  no longer making Fusion 360 the best it can be.  From day 1, y our feedback has always played a pivotal role in  the  product strategy and roadmap direction  of Fusion  360 . We’ll  continue to support you  on the journey however we can, and we whole-heartedly  appreciate your  understanding around this change.
  We thought you’d have questions for us, so we’ve compiled a comprehensive list of FAQs. You can a ccess it via the link below.
              Your local resellers are also able to help you answer any questions you may have, so feel free to  reach out to them as well.
      Subscribe to get product updates and enhancements, useful Fusion 360 tips and tutorials, roadmap updates and community stories.
title: 更改为个人使用的Fusion 360
title_en: Changes to Fusion 360 for Personal Use

对于苏珊·埃格斯(Susan Eggers)来说，20世纪60年代中期，耶鲁经济增长中心(Yale Economic Growth Center)的老板向她索要数据，开启了她在计算机科学领域的开创性职业生涯。
助理教授霍华德·帕克(Howard Pack)要求当时还是一名秘书的埃格斯编写乘法矩阵的代码。由于缺乏编程背景，她买了一本关于Fortran的书，Fortran是最近发明的编程语言，就在那个周末她读了这本书。她上瘾了。用她的话说，就是在那一刻，她意识到计算机编程在战略上非常类似于开发桥牌的进攻策略。她停止打桥牌，开始编程。
她将继续获得加州大学伯克利分校的计算机科学博士学位，并在华盛顿大学任教，在那里她因在计算机体系结构方面的突破而广受认可。
苏珊·埃格斯(Susan Eggers)1943年出生于辛辛那提，她说，在她长大的那个时候，“小女孩们被告知要被看到，不要被听到。”她在康涅狄格学院主修经济学，这是当时的一所女子学院，但由于性别的原因，她没有想到会成为一名经济学家。例如，当她申请纽黑文美林证券公司的股票经纪人时，她被告知“女孩不能当股票经纪人，但你可以申请秘书职位。”她说，这段经历打消了她对自己职业生涯的任何抱负。
她最终在EGC找到了一份秘书的工作，在那里她为EGC的创始人兼董事劳埃德·雷诺兹(Lloyd Reynolds)工作了一年。之后，她为帕克工作，帕克当时是一名助理教授，也是EGC国家研究项目的一部分。这是首批采用量化方法了解低收入国家经济的研究项目之一。帕克宿命地要求埃格斯将程序编码为矩阵相乘。
“如果他没有这样做，我可能永远不会发现计算机编程，我也永远不会成为一名计算机科学家，”埃格斯说。
凭借计算技能，她成为了一名研究助理，完成了EGC周围的数据任务。她还在大学周围的其他地方工作，包括在医学院，在那里她为第一个具有医学语义的医疗记录系统的开发做出了贡献，这意味着数据库可以将症状与潜在的诊断联系起来，并确定任何药物冲突。
埃格斯说：“这实际上是一个人工智能系统，但这个词还没有被普遍使用。”
在EGC时，她开始积极参与工会组织。她加入了非教职员工行动委员会，因为她的薪水是最低的之一-在EGC的第一年，她的年薪为3600美元-并为员工争取更高的工资和更好的雇佣做法。
工会组织带领埃格斯发起了新生的妇女运动。她在纽黑文经营着一个发言人办公室，向小学、初中和高中，以及大学、教堂和犹太教堂介绍运动的目标。
她在快30岁的时候决定去读研究生，这既是她看到妇女运动扩大了可能性的产物，也是实际考虑的结果。拥有经济学本科学位的她很难找到编程方面的工作，尽管她有丰富的经验。获得博士学位将帮助她跨越在每一份新工作中都必须证明自己的问题。
几乎在她开始讲话后，他们(她的男性同龄人)就开始大声疾呼，所以她用水喷洒他们，他们再也没有切断她的联系。
当她开始在加州大学伯克利分校工作时，埃格斯已经作为主要作者出版了两本书。他们的贡献是一种通过压缩构成绝大多数统计数据集的零来节省数据存储的方法。她的方法还包括将零带回计算数据的“挂钩”。
这些出版物是她研究生院研究的垫脚石，在研究生院，她专注于并行处理或计算机如何在不同的处理器上同时执行任务。她的论文开发了一种方法，确保每个处理器中的本地高速缓存或内存都使用来自其他处理器的最新数据进行更新，从而确保所有处理器都访问最新的数据。
尽管如此，尽管埃格斯比她在伯克利的大多数男性同龄人更有经验，但她发现他们经常在她面前说三道四，所以她想出了一个计划，一劳永逸地平息他们的打扰。在她关于她的研究的陈述中，她带来了一个水上玩具。几乎在她开始说话后，他们就开始大声说话，所以她用水喷洒他们，他们再也没有打断她。
1989年，47岁的苏珊·埃格斯(Susan Eggers)获得博士学位，进入华盛顿大学(University Of Washington)计算机科学系，即现在的艾伦计算机科学学院(Allen School Of Computer Science)，在那里她获得了微软计算机科学教授的头衔。她在华盛顿的这些年产生了几个有益的伙伴关系。她在西雅图遇到了她的丈夫、精神病学家蒂姆·凯勒(Tim Keller)，他们珍贵的婚姻支持了她的创业生涯。
埃格斯与华盛顿的同事汉克·利维(Hank Levy)共同开发了第一项商业上可行的硬件技术，该技术允许处理器同时执行来自不同线程的多条指令，从而提高了计算机硬件利用率，并将性能提高了多达四倍。该设计被英特尔(在英特尔处理器中称为超线程)、IBM、Sun和其他计算机制造商采用。由于这一突破和其他工作，埃格斯在2018年获得了计算机架构领域颁发的最高奖项-埃克特-毛利奖(Eckert-Mauchly Award)。她是第一位获此殊荣的女计算机科学家。她还于2006年当选为美国国家工程院院士，并于2013年当选为美国艺术与科学学院院士。
埃格斯于2011年退休。今天，她住在西雅图，大部分时间都花在美化景观和园艺上。她还回到了政治倡导领域，与当地组织共同力量(Common Power)合作，在全国关键州登记选民并进行投票。
丽莎·钱(Lisa Qian)是耶鲁大学(Yale College)2020年经济学毕业生，曾在经济增长中心实习。
在耶鲁大学庆祝男女同校50周年和女学生150周年之际，该大学的开拓性女学者也受到了新的关注。此外，经济增长中心将在2021年庆祝成立60周年，并希望向为世世代代铺平道路的女性致敬。这篇文章是表彰参与EGC和耶鲁经济学的女性的系列文章之一。其他文章可以在这里找到。
note_en:
For Susan Eggers, a data request from her boss at the Yale Economic Growth Center in the mid 1960s initiated her path-breaking career in computer science.
 Assistant Professor Howard Pack asked Eggers, then a secretary, to write code to multiply matrices. Lacking background in coding, she bought a book on Fortran, the recently invented programming language, and read it that very weekend. She was hooked.  In her words, that was the moment when she realized that computer programming was strategically very similar to developing an offensive strategy in bridge. She stopped playing bridge and started programming.
  She would go on to receive a Ph.D. in computer science from the University of California at Berkeley and teach at the University of Washington, where she was widely recognized for breakthroughs in computer architecture.
 Susan Eggers was born in Cincinnati in 1943 and grew up at a time when, she said, “little girls were told to be seen and not heard.” She majored in economics at Connecticut College, a women’s college at the time, but did not expect to work as an economist because of her gender. For instance, when she applied to be a stockbroker at Merrill Lynch in New Haven, she was told “girls can’t be stockbrokers, but you can apply for a secretarial position.” She said this experience discouraged any aspirations she might have had for her own career.
 She ultimately took a job as a secretary at the EGC, where for a year she assisted  Lloyd Reynolds, the EGC’s founder and director. Afterwards, she worked for Pack, an assistant professor at the time and part of the EGC’s Country Studies program. This was one of the first research programs to take a quantitative approach to understanding lower-income economies. Pack fatefully asked Eggers to code the program to multiply matrices.
 “If he hadn’t done that, I may have never discovered computer programming, and I never would have become a computer scientist,” Eggers said.
 Armed with computing skills, she became a research associate, completing data tasks around the EGC. She also worked elsewhere around the university, including at the medical school, where she contributed to the development of the first medical records system with medical semantics, which means that the database could connect symptoms with potential diagnoses and determine any drug conflicts.
 “It was actually an AI system, but the term wasn’t in common usage yet,” Eggers said.
 While at the EGC, she became active in union organizing. She joined the Non-Faculty Action Committee, because she had one of the lowest salaries – $3,600 a year her first year at the EGC – and lobbied for higher salaries and better employment practices for staff.
 Union organizing led Eggers to the nascent Women’s Movement. She ran a speaker’s bureau in New Haven, which presented the goals of the movement to elementary, middle, and high schools, as well as colleges, churches, and synagogues.
 Her decision to attend graduate school in her late thirties was both a product of the expanded possibilities she saw as a result of the Women’s Movement and practical considerations. With an undergraduate degree in economics, it was difficult to find jobs in programming, even though she had extensive experience. Earning a doctorate would help her leapfrog over having to prove herself in every new job.
 Almost immediately after she started to talk, they [her male peers] began to speak up, so she sprayed them with water and they never cut her off again.
 By the time she started at the University of California, Berkeley, Eggers had two publications as lead author under her belt. Their contribution was a method to save data storage by compressing the zeroes that made up the vast majority of statistical datasets. Her method also included “hooks” that would bring the zeros back to compute on the data.
 Those publications were stepping stones to her research in graduate school, where she focused on parallel processing or how computers execute tasks simultaneously on different processors. Her dissertation developed a method that ensured that the local cache, or memory, in each processor was updated with the latest data from the other processors, ensuring that all processors accessed the most current data.
 Still, though Eggers was more experienced than her mostly male peers at Berkeley, she found that they frequently talked over her, so she came up with a plan to quash their interruptions once and for all. To a presentation she gave on her research, she brought a water toy. Almost immediately after she started to talk, they began to speak up, so she sprayed them with water and they never cut her off again.
  At the age of 47 in 1989, Susan Eggers received her Ph.D. and joined the  University of Washington’s Computer Science Department, now the Allen School of Computer Science, where she held the Microsoft Professorship in Computer Science. Her years at Washington yielded several rewarding partnerships. She met her husband, Tim Keller, a psychiatrist, in Seattle and their treasured marriage supported her pioneering career.
 With a colleague at Washington, Hank Levy, Eggers developed the first commercially viable hardware technology that allowed processors to execute multiple instructions from different threads simultaneously, improving computer hardware utilization and boosting performance by up to four times. The design was adopted by Intel (called hyper-threading in Intel processors), IBM, Sun and other computer manufacturers. For this breakthrough and other work, Eggers received the Eckert-Mauchly Award in 2018, the top prize given in the field of computer architecture. She was the first female computer scientist to be honored with this award. She was also elected to the National Academy of Engineering in 2006 and the American Academy of Arts and Science in 2013.
 Eggers retired in 2011. Today, she lives in Seattle and spends much of her time landscaping and gardening. She has also returned to political advocacy, working with a local organization, Common Power, to register voters and get out the vote in key states across the country.
   Lisa Qian is a 2020 economics graduate of Yale College, and a former intern at the Economic Growth Center.
 As Yale celebrates the 50th anniversary of coeducation and 150th anniversary of women students at the university, the university’s pioneering female scholars are also receiving renewed attention. In addition, the Economic Growth Center celebrates its 60th anniversary in 2021 and wishes to honor the women who paved the way for successive generations. This article is one is a series to recognize women who have been a part of EGC and Yale Economics. The other articles can be found  here.
title: 20世纪60年代在耶鲁的一份工作让苏珊·埃格斯走上了计算机科学的道路
title_en: A job at Yale in the 1960s set Susan Eggers on a path in computer science

IOS 14重塑了iPhone的体验，提供了一个重大的主屏幕更新，提供了重新设计的精美小部件和应用程序库，通过应用程序剪辑使用应用程序的新方式，以及强大的消息更新。IOS14今天作为免费软件更新面市。
苹果负责软件工程的高级副总裁克雷格·费德里吉(Craig Federighi)说，“和iPhone一样，iOS对于我们如何驾驭生活和保持联系至关重要，我们正在使其在iOS 14中变得更加强大和更容易使用，主屏幕进行了有史以来最大的更新，”苹果负责软件工程的高级副总裁克雷格·费德里吉(Craig Federighi)说。“我们很高兴看到开发人员利用小工具和应用程序剪辑创造新体验的不可思议方式，我们认为客户会喜欢这些新体验。”
新的Widget可以一目了然地显示及时的信息，并且可以在任何主屏幕页面上以不同的大小添加。用户可以选择小部件的智能堆栈，它使用设备上的智能来根据时间、位置和活动显示正确的小部件。例如，用户可能在早上看到News小部件，在白天看到Calendar事件，在晚上看到他们的健身摘要。可以自定义主屏幕页面，以显示工作、娱乐、体育和其他感兴趣领域的应用程序和小部件。
在主屏幕页面的末尾是App Library，这是一个新的空间，可以自动将用户的所有应用程序组织到一个简单、易于导航的视图中，并智能地显示当前有帮助的应用程序。用户还可以隐藏主屏幕页面，以便更快地访问应用程序库。
传入的FaceTime和电话以及Siri互动采用了全新的紧凑型设计，使用户能够置身于他们正在做的事情的背景中。有了画中画支持，iPhone用户现在可以在使用另一款应用程序的同时观看视频或接听FaceTime电话。
应用程序剪辑是应用程序的一小部分，它被设计为在需要的时候被发现。APP剪辑与特定的产品或业务相关联，并在几秒钟内加载，以完成特定任务，如叫外卖或给车加油。通过扫描苹果设计的新App Clip代码，1或者通过NFC标签和二维码，或者在消息中或从Safari共享，它们可以很容易地被发现和访问，所有这些都具有应用程序应有的安全性和隐私。
消息是与朋友和家人通信的核心，现在可以更轻松地保持联系并快速访问重要消息。用户可以将对话固定在消息列表的顶部，通过提及和在线回复轻松跟上活跃的群组线索，并通过使用图像或表情符号设置集体照片来进一步自定义对话。消息中的新Memoji选项更具包容性和多样性，增加了发型、头饰、面罩等。
地图使导航和探索变得比以往任何时候都更容易，包括新的骑行方向、电动汽车路线和策展指南。骑车方向要考虑海拔，街道有多繁忙，以及沿线是否有楼梯。电动汽车路线根据当前车辆充电和充电器类型，沿计划路线添加充电站点。2指南提供了城市中值得参观的有趣地方的精心策划的列表，这些地方是由精选的可信资源创建的。导游是寻找热门景点、发现餐厅和探索知名品牌的新推荐的好方法。
现在，用户可以选择在授予应用程序位置访问权限时与应用程序开发人员共享他们的大致位置，而不是他们的精确位置，并对应用程序使用麦克风和摄像头的情况获得更高的透明度。开发者现在可以为用户提供升级现有账户以登录苹果的选项。今年晚些时候，App Store产品页面将以简单易读的形式展示开发者自我报告的隐私实践摘要。从明年初开始，所有应用程序都将被要求在跟踪之前获得用户权限。3个。
AirPods和AirPods Pro在iOS 14中获得了强大的功能。具有动态头部跟踪的空间音频为AirPods Pro带来了剧场般的体验。通过应用定向音频滤波器，并微妙地调整每只耳朵接收的频率，几乎可以将声音放置在空间的任何地方，以提供身临其境的聆听体验。自动设备切换功能可在iPhone、iPad和Mac之间无缝切换AirPods和AirPods Pro音频。4耳机功能，这是一项新的无障碍功能，可以放大柔和的声音并调整音频，帮助音乐、电影、电话和播客听起来更清晰、更清晰。5个。
翻译旨在成为翻译对话的最好、最简单的应用程序，提供11种不同语言之间的语音和文本的快速自然翻译。6设备上模式允许用户离线体验应用程序的功能，进行私人语音和文本翻译。
Siri扩展了它的知识，并用来自互联网的信息回答复杂的问题。在口述信息、笔记、电子邮件等时，键盘听写可在设备上运行。7个。
家庭应用程序通过新的自动化建议和控制中心中的扩展控件，使智能家居控制变得更加容易，以便更快地访问附件和场景。兼容HomeKit灯的自适应照明全天自动调整色温，兼容视频门铃和摄像头中的人脸识别使用来自Apple TV或HomePod的设备上智能来识别访客。Home应用程序和HomeKit被设计成私密和安全的，所以关于用户家居配件的所有信息都是端到端加密的。
数字版本的汽车钥匙为用户提供了一种使用iPhone解锁和启动汽车的安全方式。汽车钥匙可以很容易地通过消息共享，或者在设备丢失时通过iCloud禁用，现在可以供使用NFC技术的兼容汽车使用。8个。
Safari提供隐私报告，这样用户就可以很容易地看到哪些跨站点追踪器被屏蔽了，安全的密码监控可以帮助用户检测保存的密码，这些密码可能涉及到数据泄露，以及整个网页的内置翻译。9个。
Health拥有全新的体验来管理睡眠，并更好地了解可能影响听力健康的音频水平。一个新的健康检查表，包括紧急SOS、医疗ID、ECG、跌倒检测等，为用户提供了一个集中管理健康和安全功能的地方。10 Health还增加了对移动性、健康记录、症状和ECG的新数据类型的支持。
Weather应用程序让用户了解最新的恶劣天气事件，当天气预报中有雨时，新的下一小时降雨图显示每分钟的降水量。11个。
Apple One是以极高的价值获得许多苹果订阅服务的最简单的方式，因此客户可以选择一个适合他们或他们整个家庭的计划。从今年秋天开始，顾客可以注册Apple One。
辅助功能包括Back Tap，它提供了触发手势、任务和辅助功能快捷方式的快速新方法；Group FaceTime中的手语检测；以及声音识别，它使用设备上的智能在检测到火警或门铃的声音时通知用户。VoiceOver是业内领先的面向盲人社区的屏幕阅读器，现在它可以自动识别屏幕上直观显示的内容，这样更多的人就可以访问更多的应用程序和网络体验。
跟踪是指出于有针对性的广告或广告测量目的，将从应用程序收集的用户或设备数据与从其他公司的应用程序、网站或离线资产收集的用户或设备数据进行链接的行为。跟踪还指与数据代理共享用户或设备数据。
可与AirPods Pro、AirPods(第二代)、PowerBeats、PowerBeats Pro和Beats Solo Pro配合使用。需要iCloud帐户。
配备H1耳机芯片的Apple和Beats耳机以及EarPods都提供耳机功能。
翻译可用于英语、普通话、日语、韩语、西班牙语、德语、法语、意大利语、俄语、巴西葡萄牙语和阿拉伯语。
在iPhone Xs及更高版本上提供英语(美国、英国、印度、加拿大、澳大利亚)、普通话(中国大陆)、粤语(香港)、日语(日本)、法语(法国)、德语(德国)和西班牙语(美国、西班牙、墨西哥)版本。需要下载听写和模型。
通过参与制造商在iPhone XR、iPhone Xs和更高版本、iPhone SE(第二代)和Apple Watch Series 5上提供。
Safari翻译提供测试版，支持英语、简体中文、西班牙语、德语、法语、俄语和巴西葡萄牙语。
包括ECG和跌倒检测在内的一些功能需要Apple Watch，有些功能可能并不是在所有地区都可以使用。
美国、欧洲、日本、加拿大和澳大利亚有恶劣天气信息，美国有下一小时降雨图。
note_en:
iOS 14 reimagines the iPhone experience, delivering a major update to the Home Screen with beautifully redesigned widgets and the App Library, new ways to use apps with App Clips, and powerful updates to Messages.  iOS 14 is available today as a free software update.
  “Together with iPhone, iOS is central to how we navigate our lives and stay connected, and we are making it even more powerful and easier to use in iOS 14, with the biggest update ever to the Home Screen,” said Craig Federighi, Apple’s senior vice president of Software Engineering. “We are thrilled to see the incredible ways developers are taking advantage of widgets and App Clips to create new experiences that we think customers are going to love.”
  New widgets present timely information at a glance and can be added in different sizes on any Home Screen page. Users can choose a Smart Stack of widgets, which uses on-device intelligence to surface the right widget based on time, location, and activity. For example, users might see the News widget in the morning, Calendar events during the day, and their Fitness summary in the evening. Home Screen pages can be customized to show apps and widgets for work, entertainment, sports, and other areas of interest.
  At the end of the Home Screen pages is the App Library, a new space that automatically organizes all of a user’s apps into one simple, easy-to-navigate view, and intelligently surfaces apps that are helpful in the moment. Users can also hide Home Screen pages for quicker access to the App Library.
    Incoming FaceTime and phone calls and Siri interactions take on an all-new compact design that enables users to stay in the context of what they are doing. With Picture-in-Picture support, iPhone users can now watch a video or take a FaceTime call while using another app.
    An App Clip is a small part of an app that is designed to be discovered the moment it is needed. App Clips are associated with a particular product or business, and load within seconds to complete a specific task, such as ordering takeout or filling up the car with gas. They can be easily discovered and accessed by scanning a new Apple-designed App Clip code, 1 or through NFC tags and QR codes, or shared in Messages or from Safari, all with the security and privacy expected from apps.
    Messages is central to communicating with friends and family, and now it’s easier to stay connected and quickly access important messages. Users can pin conversations to the top of their messages list, easily keep up with lively group threads through mentions and inline replies, and further customize conversations by setting a group photo using an image or emoji. New Memoji options in Messages are even more inclusive and diverse with additional hairstyles, headwear, face coverings, and more.
    Maps makes it easier than ever to navigate and explore with new cycling directions, electric vehicle routing, and curated Guides. Cycling directions take into account elevation, how busy a street is, and whether there are stairs along the route. Electric vehicle routing adds charging stops along a planned route based on current vehicle charge and charger types. 2 Guides provide a curated list of interesting places to visit in a city, created by a selection of trusted resources. Guides are a great way to find popular attractions, discover restaurants, and explore new recommendations from respected brands.
    Now users can choose to share their approximate location with app developers rather than their precise location when granting an app location access, and get even more transparency into an app’s use of the microphone and camera. Developers can now offer users the option to upgrade their existing accounts to Sign in with Apple. Later this year, App Store product pages will feature summaries of developers’ self-reported privacy practices, displayed in a simple, easy-to-read format. Starting early next year, all apps will be required to obtain user permission before tracking. 3
    AirPods and AirPods Pro gain powerful capabilities with iOS 14. Spatial audio with dynamic head tracking brings a theater-like experience to AirPods Pro. By applying directional audio filters, and subtly adjusting the frequencies each ear receives, sounds can be placed virtually anywhere in a space to provide an immersive listening experience. Automatic device switching seamlessly switches AirPods and AirPods Pro audio between iPhone, iPad, and Mac. 4 Headphone accommodations, a new accessibility feature, amplifies soft sounds and tunes audio to help music, movies, phone calls, and podcasts sound crisper and clearer. 5
    Translate is designed to be the best and easiest app for translating conversations, offering quick and natural translation of voice and text among 11 different languages. 6 On-device mode allows users to experience the features of the app offline for private voice and text translation.
  Siri expands its knowledge and answers complex questions with information sourced from across the internet. Keyboard dictation runs on device when dictating messages, notes, email, and more. 7
  The  Home app makes smart home control even easier with new automation suggestions and expanded controls in Control Center for quicker access to accessories and scenes. Adaptive Lighting for compatible HomeKit-enabled lights automatically adjusts the color temperature throughout the day, and Face Recognition in compatible video doorbells and cameras uses on-device intelligence from Apple TV or HomePod to identify visitors. The Home app and HomeKit are built to be private and secure, so all information about a user’s home accessories is end-to-end encrypted.
  Digital versions of  car keys give users a secure way to use iPhone to unlock and start their car. Car keys can be easily shared using Messages, or disabled through iCloud if a device is lost, and are available today for compatible cars using NFC technology. 8
  Safari offers a Privacy Report so users can easily see which cross-site trackers have been blocked, secure password monitoring to help users detect saved passwords that may have been involved in a data breach, and built-in translation for entire webpages. 9
  Health has all-new experiences to manage sleep and better understand audio levels that may affect hearing health. A new Health Checklist that includes Emergency SOS, Medical ID, ECG, fall detection, and more offers users a centralized place to manage health and safety features. 10 Health also adds support for new data types for mobility, Health Records, symptoms, and ECG.
  The  Weather app keeps users up to date on severe weather events, and a new next-hour precipitation chart shows minute-by-minute precipitation when rain is in the forecast. 11
  Apple One is the easiest way to get many Apple subscription services at a great value, so customers can choose the one plan  that is right for them or their whole family. Customers can sign up for Apple One starting this fall.
  Accessibility features include Back Tap, which offers a quick new way to trigger gestures, tasks, and accessibility shortcuts, sign language detection in Group FaceTime, and Sound Recognition, which uses on-device intelligence to notify users when the sound of a fire alarm or doorbell is detected. VoiceOver, the industry’s leading screen reader for the blind community, now automatically recognizes what is displayed visually onscreen so more apps and web experiences are accessible to more people.
          Tracking refers to the act of linking user or device data collected from an app with user or device data collected from other companies’ apps, websites, or offline properties for targeted advertising or advertising measurement purposes. Tracking also refers to sharing user or device data with data brokers.
 Works with AirPods Pro, AirPods (2nd generation), Powerbeats, Powerbeats Pro, and Beats Solo Pro. Requires an iCloud account.
 Headphone Accommodations is available on Apple and Beats headphones featuring the H1 headphone chip, as well as EarPods.
 Translate is available for English, Mandarin Chinese, Japanese, Korean, Spanish, German, French, Italian, Russian, Brazilian Portuguese, and Arabic.
 Available on iPhone Xs and later in English (U.S., UK, India, Canada, Australia), Mandarin Chinese (China mainland), Cantonese (Hong Kong), Japanese (Japan), French (France), German (Germany), and Spanish (U.S., Spain, Mexico). Requires download of dictation models.
 Available on iPhone Xr, iPhone Xs and later, iPhone SE (2nd generation) and Apple Watch Series 5 through participating manufacturers.
 Safari translation is available in beta supporting English, Simplified Chinese, Spanish, German, French, Russian, and Brazilian Portuguese.
 Some features, including ECG and fall detection, require Apple Watch, and some features may not be available in all regions.
 Severe weather information is available for locations in the US, Europe, Japan, Canada, and Australia, and the next-hour precipitation chart is available in the US.

制作跨平台应用程序有很好的理由--首先，它们是跨平台的--但这不是我们的本色。我们成立于1997年，是一家Mac软件公司，我们对恐慌的喜悦来自于创造出感觉真的很像Mac的东西。
很久以前，我们创建了Coda，这是一个全新的Mac网络编辑器。但当我们开始研究Nova的时候，我们看到了网络今天在哪里，以及我们需要在哪里。现在是重新开始的时候了。
它是全新的、超快的、灵活的，拥有你想要的所有功能：智能自动补全、多光标、小地图、编辑器滚动、标签对和方括号，等等。
对于好奇的用户，Nova内置了对CoffeeScript、CSS、DIFF、ERB、HAML、HTML、INI、JavaScript、JSON、JSX、LESS、LUA、Markdown、Perl、PHP、Python、Ruby、Sass、SCSS、Smarty、SQL、TSX、TypeScript、XML和YAML的支持。
(下面是一个编辑的小故事，好玩。在测试期间，我们在苹果的文本布局引擎中发现了一些我们无法修复的错误。我们的解决方案？编写我们自己的文本布局管理器…。从头开始。这不仅修复了错误，还提升了我们编辑的表现。我们不是在胡闹！)。
但是，即使是世界上最好的文本引擎也毫无意义，除非你真的很享受花时间在这款应用上。那么，诺娃看起来怎么样？
你可以让Nova看起来完全像你想要的那样，同时仍然感觉像Mac一样。聪明的，黑暗的，赛博朋克，全靠你。另外，主题类似于CSS并且易于编写。当您的Mac从浅色模式切换到深色模式时，Nova甚至可以自动更改您的主题。
您可以轻松地为项目创建生成和运行任务。我们在尾巴没有它们，但是孩子们，我们现在有了。它们是可以随时通过工具栏按钮或键盘快捷键触发的自定义脚本。
想象一下构建内容，只需单击一个按钮即可看到Nova启动您的本地服务器，获取适当的URL，并立即为您打开浏览器。只要想想你能节省的时间就行了。
Nova支持单独的构建、运行和清理任务。它可以在运行时打开报告。并且脚本可以用多种语言编写。
我们捆绑了非常有用的工具来帮助您快速高效地完成工作。当然，他们速度都很快，也都是土生土长的。
“新建选项卡”按钮不只是打开一个新文档。尽管它也会这么做。
点击它可以快速访问功能丰富的传输文件浏览器，或超级方便的提示终端，就在Nova内部。
侧边栏也可以拆分，一次在编辑器的左侧和/或右侧显示多个工具。您还可以将喜欢的工具拖放到顶部的侧边栏停靠栏中，只需单击一下即可访问。
Nova还内置了Git源代码控制工具。克隆。单击以克隆。初始化回购。拿来拉过去。上台和下台。承诺。推。你知道规矩的。(我们还没有内置的diff，但它在我们的清单上！)。
Git状态在编辑器和侧边栏中都可用。还有一个有用的&#34；Show Last change for Line&#34；弹出窗口解释提交。
Nova扩展可以做很多事情，比如添加对新语言的支持、扩展侧栏、绘制漂亮的新主题和语法颜色、验证不同的代码等等。
更棒的是，扩展是用JavaScript编写的，因此任何人都可以编写它们。Nova包含内置扩展模板，可实现快速开发。
我们是来帮忙的。Nova有一整套的设置。我们拥有易于定制的密钥绑定。我们有自定义的、可快速切换的工作空间布局。我们有大量的编辑器调整，从匹配的括号到滚动。
(如果您需要做一些Nova没有的工作，请告诉我们！)。新星一直在变化，一直在增长。)
note_en:
There are great reasons to make cross-platform apps — to start,				they're cross-platform — but it's just not who we are. Founded				as a Mac software company in 1997, our joy at Panic comes from				building things that feel truly, well, Mac-like.
  Long ago, we created Coda, an all-in-one Mac web editor that broke				new ground. But when we started work on Nova, we looked at where the				web was today, and where we needed to be. It was time for a fresh				start.
    It's new, hyper-fast, and flexible, with all the features you want:				smart autocomplete, multiple cursors, a Minimap, editor overscroll,				tag pairs and brackets, and way, way more.
    For the curious, Nova has built-in support for  CoffeeScript,  CSS,  Diff,  ERB,  Haml,  HTML,  INI,  JavaScript,  JSON,  JSX,  Less,  Lua,  Markdown,  Perl,  PHP,  Python,  Ruby,  Sass,  SCSS,  Smarty,  SQL,  TSX,  TypeScript,  XML, and  YAML.
    (Here's a little editor story for fun. During beta we found some				bugs in Apple's text layout engine that we just could not fix. Our				solution? Writing our own text layout manager… from scratch.				Not only did this fix the bugs, but it also boosted our editor's				performance. We're not messing around!)
  But even the best text engine in the world means nothing unless you				actually enjoy spending your time in the app. So, how does Nova				look?
    You can make Nova look exactly the way you want, while still feeling				Mac-like. Bright, dark, cyberpunk, it's all you. Plus, themes are				CSS-like and easy to write. Nova can even automatically change your				theme when your Mac switches from light to dark mode.
    You can easily create build and run tasks for your projects. We				didn't have them in Coda, but boy do we have them now. They're				custom scripts that can be triggered at any time by toolbar buttons				or keyboard shortcuts.
  Imagine building content, and with the single click of a button				watching as Nova fires up your local server, grabs the appropriate				URL, and opens a browser for you, instantly. Just think of the time				you'll save.
  Nova supports separate  Build,  Run,				and  Clean tasks. It can open a report when run. And				the scripts can be written in a variety of languages.
    We've bundled in extremely useful tools to help you get your work				done quickly and efficiently. They're all fast and native too, of				course.
    The  New Tab button doesn't just open a fresh				document. although it does that, too.
  Click it to quickly access a feature-packed  Transmit file				browser, or a super-convenient  Prompt				terminal, all right inside Nova.
      The sidebar can also be split to show multiple tools at once, on the				left and/or right side of your editor. And you can drag your				favorite tools into the sidebar dock at the top for one-click				access.
    Nova also has  Git source control tools built-in.				Clone. Click-to-clone. Initialize a repo. Fetch and pull. Stage and				unstage. Commit. Push. You know the drill. (We don't have built-in				diff yet, but it's on our list!)
  Git status is available both in the editor and the sidebar. And a				useful "Show Last Change for Line" pop-up explains commits.
    A Nova extension can do lots of things, like add support for new				languages, extend the sidebar, draw beautiful new themes and syntax				colors, validate different code, and much more.
  Even better, extensions are written in JavaScript, so anyone can				write them. And Nova includes built-in extension templates for fast				development.
    And we're here to help. Nova has a whole host of settings. We have				easily customizable key bindings. We have custom, quickly-switchable				workspace layouts. And we have loads of editor tweaks, from matching				brackets to overscroll.
  (And if there's something you need to work that Nova doesn't have,				just let us know! Nova is always changing, always growing.)
